summary: Partially decompiled the common fighter FlyReflect code by adding src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_FlyReflect.c and a dedicated header, removing most of the corresponding assembly from asm/melee/ft/ft_0C08.s, and wiring the new object files into obj_files.mk. The PR report shows the new C file at 98.45% overall, with ftCo_800C15F4, ftCo_800C1718, ftCo_FlyReflect_Anim, ftCo_FlyReflect_IASA, and ftCo_FlyReflect_Phys at 100%, while ftCo_800C17CC, ftCo_FlyReflect_Coll, and ftCo_800C18A8 remained partial. It also added typed surface/motion-var/common-data fields needed to express collision reflection behavior.
what_changed: Added a new ftCo_FlyReflect.c implementation containing C versions or guarded partial C versions for ftCo_800C15F4, ftCo_800C1718, ftCo_800C17CC, ftCo_800C18A8, ftCo_FlyReflect_Anim, ftCo_FlyReflect_IASA, ftCo_FlyReflect_Phys, and ftCo_FlyReflect_Coll.
Removed the FlyReflect function bodies from asm/melee/ft/ft_0C08.s and left ft_0C08.s to continue carrying later unmatched code.
Added asm/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_FlyReflect.s for FlyReflect literals in .sdata2: 0.0, -1.0, 1.0, 0.5, and 3.0.
Added asm/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Bury.s for related static data/literals that were apparently needed after object splitting, including jobj assert strings and float/double constants.
Added ftCo_FlyReflect.h and moved FlyReflect prototypes out of src/melee/ft/ft_0C08.h into the dedicated header.
Included ftCo_FlyReflect.h from ftCo_Damage.c, ftCo_DownDamage.c, and ftCo_Init.c so existing users resolve against the new header.
Added ftCo_Surface enum values in ftCommon/forward.h: FTCO_Surface_None, ftCo_Surface_LeftWall, ftCo_Surface_RightWall, and ftCo_Surface_Ceiling.
Refined ftCommon_MotionVars.damage by splitting the previous x18 byte array into explicit u8 x18 and u8 x19 fields at fp+2358 and fp+2359.
Refined ftCommonData field types at offsets x1B0, x1B8, x1BC, and x1C0 to float/int/float/float, matching their use as reflect thresholds, collision parameter, velocity scaling, and timer data.
Changed ft_80081F2C and ft_80082084 declarations/definitions from unknown or void signatures to bool-returning functions taking Fighter_GObj*, enabling their use in FlyReflect collision checks.
Changed ftKb_SpecialN_800F1F1C's gobj parameter type from ftKb_GObj* to ftCo_GObj*, matching its use from common FlyReflect code.
Added the new C and asm object files to obj_files.mk so the split source/literal files participate in the build.
smart_moves: Separated fully matched helper functions from still-problematic functions: the report shows several 100% functions while keeping ftCo_800C17CC, ftCo_FlyReflect_Coll, and ftCo_800C18A8 under ASM/MUST_MATCH fallback blocks.
Used dedicated literal externs and companion .sdata2 assembly files to preserve exact constant placement while moving logic into C.
Introduced ftCo_Surface as a named enum instead of leaving magic byte values for the last reflected surface, making repeated-wall/ceiling suppression understandable.
Split damage motion variable bytes x18 and x19 so the reflect cooldown/timer and last-surface state can be expressed directly in C.
Typed ftCommonData fields only where this PR had concrete usage evidence, avoiding a broad speculative struct cleanup.
Reused existing vector/collision helpers directly in the C reconstruction: lbVector_Add_xy, lbVector_Mirror, ft_80081F2C, ft_80082084, ftColl_8007B760, Camera_80030E44, and efAsync_Spawn.
Kept decomp progress buildable by adding both the new C object and the asm literal/data objects while leaving remaining unmatched assembly guarded in source.
decomp_lessons: When extracting functions from a monolithic asm file, check whether local literals or neighboring data must move into a new per-feature asm file to preserve section order and addresses.
Partial matches can still be valuable if exact C is used for easy leaf/state functions and ASM fallbacks remain for hard control-flow or codegen-sensitive functions.

Collision-state functions often require struct typing across several layers: fighter motion vars, common data, collision flags, and helper function signatures all had to be improved before the C became readable.
Named enum values for byte-sized state fields are useful even before all surrounding symbols are known; here they document left wall, right wall, and ceiling reflect suppression.
Guarding unused-stack padding under MUST_MATCH documents the codegen issue without polluting non-matching/readable builds.
Helper signatures should be updated from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS only when call-site evidence is strong; this PR used FlyReflect call sites to type ft_80081F2C, ft_80082084, and ftKb_SpecialN_800F1F1C.
A decomp PR may need header include cleanup in consumers when moving prototypes out of an aggregate header such as ft_0C08.h into a feature-specific header.
naming_conventions: Feature source/header names follow the common fighter motion style: ftCo_FlyReflect.c and ftCo_FlyReflect.h.
Action callbacks use the motion suffix convention: ftCo_FlyReflect_Anim, ftCo_FlyReflect_IASA, ftCo_FlyReflect_Phys, and ftCo_FlyReflect_Coll.
Unknown address-based helpers remain named by address, such as ftCo_800C15F4, ftCo_800C1718, ftCo_800C17CC, and ftCo_800C18A8.
The new enum type is ftCo_Surface, with values for ftCo_Surface_LeftWall, ftCo_Surface_RightWall, and ftCo_Surface_Ceiling; the zero value is spelled FTCO_Surface_None in the diff.
Common-data fields remained offset-derived names, e.g. x1B0, x1B8, x1BC, and x1C0, but gained concrete C types.
Motion variable fields retained offset-derived names x18 and x19 once their byte layout was identified.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Left difficult functions wrapped as ASM functions with C bodies enabled when !MUST_MATCH or WIP, and inline assembly fallback under MUST_MATCH.
Used #pragma force_active on under WIP around literal externs to help preserve constant/data emission during matching experiments.
Declared file-local literal symbols as extern constants and supplied their exact values in asm/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_FlyReflect.s.
Kept explicit unused stack padding under MUST_MATCH, e.g. u8 _[8] and u8 _[4], to account for stack layout differences in matched builds.
Used the project RETURN_IF macro in the readable ftCo_FlyReflect_Coll C path to mirror early-return collision sequencing compactly.
Replaced an assembly sqrt/frsqrte sequence in the readable C path with sqrtf(SQ(vel_x) + SQ(vel_y)) while preserving the inline asm fallback for exact matching.
Object-file ordering was updated to place ftCo_Bury.c, ftCo_Bury.s, ftCo_FlyReflect.c, ftCo_FlyReflect.s, then the remaining ft_0C08.s, indicating careful section/order management.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human text excerpt is the automated/report-style match table for src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_FlyReflect.c.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that fully matched ftCo_800C17CC, ftCo_FlyReflect_Coll, or ftCo_800C18A8.
Search for later renames or cleanups of ftCo_Surface and FTCO_Surface_None casing.
Check whether ftCommonData x1B0, x1B8, x1BC, and x1C0 received semantic names after this PR.
Look for subsequent PRs that remove ASM fallbacks from ftCo_FlyReflect.c.
Search for other PRs that split monolithic ft_0C08.s into feature-specific ftCommon source files and asm literal files.
